attributes.content:
## Overview

The Payslip card gives the workspace a structured home for pay statement with gross, deductions, net, period, and breakdown.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Payslip acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Contact]]** -- Payslip often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Organization]]** -- Payslip frequently sits inside an account, company, or team-level relationship context.
- **[[Task]]** -- Payslip often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Payslip can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Document]]** -- Payslip can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Interview Feedback]]** -- Interview Feedback can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Payslip.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Payslip as the structured home for pay statement with gross, deductions, net, period, and breakdown and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Payslip when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Payslip stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
